prerequisites: A Factorial account with admin-level access, since API Keys require full admin permissions and are created from the Advanced section of the company Settings.
An API key generated in Factorial (Settings > Advanced > API Keys), used as the api_key secret.
A start_date value to bound the records the connector pulls (required config field).
Optionally, a limit value to cap the number of records returned (optional config field).

authMethods.name: API Key
authMethods.summary: Factorial's public API authenticates requests with an API Key passed in the x-api-key request header. This connector's single api_key secret field holds that key. Per Factorial, API keys have total access to the account and never expire, so store the key securely. Factorial notes API Key usage is supported for internal company integrations, while OAuth2 is required for marketplace integrations; this connector uses the API Key method.

setupSteps.title: Create an API key in Factorial
setupSteps.body: Sign in to Factorial as an administrator. Go to Settings, scroll to the Advanced section, and select API Keys. Click Create new API Key, enter a name, and click Get my API key. Copy the generated key, since it grants full access to the account.

setupSteps.title: Provide the API key to the connector
setupSteps.body: Paste the generated key into the api_key field. The connector sends it to Factorial's API in the x-api-key header to authenticate requests.

setupSteps.title: Set the sync window and optional limit
setupSteps.body: Set the required start_date field to define the earliest data the connector should retrieve. Optionally set the limit field to control the maximum number of records returned.

sources.title: Factorial Help Center - How to Create API Keys
